response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned software engineer with 15+ years of experience and genuine systems-programming depth (low-latency DLang services, async I/O, high-throughput microservices), but the role demands current, hands-on C++/STL expertise for industrial automation. His C++ experience is dated (National Instruments, 2014–2015) and brief, and there is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at the required depth, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial cybersecurity. The recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js web product work, which maps poorly to this position's core requirements.
strengths: Strong systems-programming background (low-latency, async I/O, fibers, high-performance services at millions of requests/sec)
Broad, long-tenured engineering career with TDD, CI/CD, and mentoring/teaching experience
Experience with event-driven architectures and distributed platforms (GISCollective, Kubernetes deployments)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or OT-protocol experience evident
No demonstrated Azure or industrial IoT exposure; domain gap would be significant

key_factors.factor: Solid low-latency and concurrent systems experience (transferable)
